editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter to John Moorhead regarding interviewing witnesses for his case.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
The King v. Ellen Davidson of Taughey Please note Mrs Davidson's
address is Taughey, and
not Faughey Customs & Excise
Ballymoney 29 May 1916 Dear Sir, I have interviewed the witnesses in the
above case to-day and the evidence given by
them is as follows:— (1st) Mrs Ellen Boyd of Drumshaglis states that
the soldier was living with her since before the beginning
of July 1915, as she remembers that on the 12th
or 13th July 1915 (when there were holidays at the
Balnamore mill at which the soldier worked) she
went out to visit a neighbour and on her
return found that the soldier had gone fishing
to the River Bann & had locked the door, so
that she was unable to get into her house
until his return, & she is positive that she had
been living with her beforesome weeks before this. (2nd) Mrs Margaret Graham of Drumshaglis (who
lives next door to Mrs Boyd) corroborates the above
statement and is more definite in her evidence
than (3rd) William Hugh Hackett of Drumshaglis
who states that he is certain that the soldier
lived with Mrs Boyd for at any rate 3 or 4
months before and up to the date of enlistment.
As Hackett is in a better position than either of
the others & can have no possible interest in the
case, it might be advisable to have him also Yours truly MacWarnock John R Moorhead Esq
Rex. V Ellen Davidson
WITNESSES FOR PROSECUTION 1 THOMAS McAFEE J. P. Witnessed the signature of
defendant to Army Form 0.1839 ( Declaration
in respect of her son Ptve, Hugh Reid) on
20th. December 1915. 2. R%A.WARNOCK Pension Officer Ballymoney Made enquiries and
found out that the answers in defendant's Declaration
at 4a. viz: — "wasthe Soldier living with you
"immediately before he enlisted" Answer "Yes"
is false as the Soldier had been living with
his Grandmother (Mrs.Ellen Boyd) since before
July 1915. 3. Mrs. Ellen Boyd, the Grandmother will corroborate 4. Mrs Margaret Graham: a neighbour of Mrs. Boyd will also
corroborate. 5. Wm. Hugh Hackett, Farmer, who can have no possible
interest in the case, will also corroborate.
